root_motivation: Heavy Rust compile and CPU jobs (autobuilder cold-builds, wake-word retraining) OOM-kill or pin all cores on this no-swap laptop. The user needs a zero-mesh rung today — ssh + sccache + config file — that offloads those jobs to a dedicated cloud box without requiring the full NATS fleet infrastructure. This is the on-ramp that the full constellation fleet will eventually graduate from.
user_persona: Joe Yen, solo developer running Claude Code + voice stack on a no-swap laptop; wants to burst heavy Rust compiles and ML CPU jobs to a cheap dedicated Hetzner cloud box without standing up a full fleet.

acceptance_criteria.description: wm-burst init writes a valid config.toml (remote host, sccache endpoint+bucket, monthly budget cap, optional pod provider) and wm-burst init --show round-trips it; a missing/invalid config produces a clear, actionable error.

wm-burst provision emits an idempotent Ansible playbook (or playbook invocation) that takes a fresh host to a ready builder (pinned 1.85+1.88 toolchains, sccache, shared-cache creds) — demonstrated against a real host OR with a dry-run/check-mode pass that succeeds.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/mocks/ac2.rs (mock: playbook generation + structural validation; deferred for real-host dry-run)

acceptance_criteria.description: wm-burst doctor hard-fails when remote rustc set does not match rust-toolchain.toml, printing exact local-vs-remote mismatch, and passes when they match — proven by a unit/integration test that feeds a mismatched toolchain pair and asserts non-zero exit with diagnostic.
wm-burst build compiles a target crate with RUSTC_WRAPPER=sccache against shared cache and reports where it ran + cache hit/miss counts; a second build shows materially higher cache-hit ratio.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/mocks/ac4.rs (mock: sccache stats reporting; deferred for real remote build)

acceptance_criteria.description: wm-burst exec -- <cmd> runs a non-cargo CPU job on the remote box and streams its output + exit code locally; the exit status is faithfully propagated.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/mocks/ac5.rs (mock: exec command builder + exit-code propagation; deferred for real SSH)

acceptance_criteria.description: Pod tier: wm-burst pod up|down creates an ephemeral builder, runs a job, tears it down on completion/idle-timeout; full lifecycle + cost estimate appended to cost log; monthly burst-budget cap enforced (exceeding blocks new pods, no surprise bill). Provable with mocked provider.
wm-burst status reports standing box load, cache hit rate, month-to-date burst spend vs cap, and last N jobs (where each ran, duration, cost).
CLI hygiene: sigpipe::reset() is first line of main(), --help/--version work, MSRV 1.85, no let-chains; wm-burst status | head does not coredump.

scope: wm-burst init: write/edit ~/.config/wm-burst/config.toml with remote host, sccache endpoint+bucket, monthly budget cap, optional pod provider
wm-burst provision: generate and invoke an Ansible playbook to converge a fresh host to builder-ready
wm-burst doctor: verify remote reachability, toolchain match vs rust-toolchain.toml, sccache bucket writability, and cache hit rate
wm-burst build [-- <cargo args>]: run build with RUSTC_WRAPPER=sccache against shared cache and remote builder; stream output; report where it ran + hit/miss counts + elapsed
wm-burst exec -- <cmd>: run a non-cargo CPU job on remote box, stream output+exit-code locally
wm-burst pod up|down: ephemeral high-core/GPU pod via configured provider; auto-teardown; cost log; budget cap enforcement
wm-burst status: show standing box load, cache hit rate, month-to-date burst spend vs cap, last N jobs
non_goals: NATS work-queue / capability registry / fleet routing (constellation-dispatch and constellation-cloud-build own those)
Voice stack (real-time, must stay local, never bursts)
Automatic hardware procurement / cloud account setup

additional.sigpipe_reset: sigpipe::reset() must be first line of main() per self_sigpipe_panic_toolkit memory
additional.no_let_chains: no let-chain syntax (MSRV 1.85 does not stabilize let-chains)

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the user need a cloud burst CLI?
five_whys_trace.a: Heavy Rust compiles and wake-word retraining OOM-kill or pin all cores on a no-swap laptop, blocking day-to-day work.

five_whys_trace.q: Why can't the existing fleet infrastructure (constellation-cloud-build) solve this today?
five_whys_trace.a: constellation-cloud-build requires NATS mesh + JetStream work-queue + capability registry — none of which are live yet; it's gated on multiple other PRDs.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does a no-mesh rung need a dedicated CLI rather than just a shell alias?
five_whys_trace.a: Toolchain drift (1.85/1.88 split) has caused spurious compile failures before; a CLI can hard-fail on mismatch before silently corrupting a build. A shell alias can't enforce that guardrail.

five_whys_trace.q: Why add pod management when a standing dedicated box already solves the main problem?
five_whys_trace.a: Some jobs (e.g. full ML retraining with GPU) are too large even for the dedicated box; pods let the user burst to larger capacity for one-off jobs without buying permanent hardware, while the monthly cap prevents surprise bills.

ambiguities_resolved.question: sccache-dist vs remote-cargo-over-ssh for v0.1?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: AC4 explicitly allows either; the mock tests the reporting/stats surface, not the specific transport. Real implementation can use either path.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Shared-cache backend: Hetzner Object Storage vs B2 vs MinIO?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Configurable at init time; default in config schema is MinIO on the dedicated box (cheapest for something you already pay for). The sccache endpoint is a URL so any S3-compatible backend works.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What is the crate binary name?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: The PRD names the binary wm-burst; the crate slug for the wintermute repo is constellation-burst-builder per the build tick. The Cargo.toml [package] name is wm-burst, [[bin]] name = 'wm-burst'.
